title: Lasiarrhenum trinervium
intro: Lasiarrhenum trinervium, commonly known as the Three-Nerved Lasiarrhenum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Lasiarrhenum trinervium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to support wound healing and skin health.
properties: Lasiarrhenum trinervium is reported to have been used in traditional medicine, although limited information is available on its specific therapeutic properties. However, its purported uses include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant may be used to treat various health conditions, such as wounds and skin irritations. Further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects. Its traditional uses may vary depending on the region and cultural context.

preparations: Lasiarrhenum trinervium, a plant native to the Western Ghats of India, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. Herbal preparations of Lasiarrhenum trinervium are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and roots are typically harvested and dried to create teas, infusions, and decoctions that are used to treat various health issues.
